title: Steak Pie
excerpt: Ingredients 80 ml coarsely ground pepper 1 kg fillet or aged rump 30 ml oil 40 ml brandy 125 ml thick cream 100 ml stock 80 g soft green peppercorns Salt and pepper to taste one roll of ready-made puff pastry 1 egg, beaten Method Place the coarsely ground pepper on a wooden board, and roll …
html:
Ingredients

  • 80 ml coarsely ground pepper
  • 1 kg fillet or aged rump
  • 30 ml oil
  • 40 ml brandy
  • 125 ml thick cream
  • 100 ml stock
  • 80 g soft green peppercorns
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • one roll of ready-made puff pastry
  • 1 egg, beaten

Method

Place the coarsely ground pepper on a wooden board, and roll the steak in the pepper to form a crust. Cut the steak into strips. Place the oil in a pan, heat and lightly fry the steak strips until browned. Remove from the pan, cover with foil and let it rest.

Pour the brandy into the same pan and let it cook for a bit until the bits of leftover meat come loose. Add the cream, stock and green peppercorns to the brandy and let it reduce until it thickens. Season to taste.

Place the steak strips back into the sauce and let it cook through for about a minute. Set aside to cool.

Roll out the puff pastry on a smooth surface, place a plate upside down on the pastry, and cut out a circle of pastry. Spoon the meat mixture onto one half of the circle, fold the other half over the meat mixture so that you have a half circle, and press the edges closed with a fork. Brush the egg over the pastry and bake at 180ºC until the pastry is nicely browned.

Easy!
